reasoning: The document shows a critical fidelity failure with an explicit omission of 34 sections due to token budget limits, resulting in a low fidelity score of 0.4. Despite programmatic word count ratios appearing acceptable (0.92), the specific identification of missing core sections and truncated acronym lists creates a major conflict between signal sources that requires human intervention.

concerns.analysis: The output is missing a vast majority of the source document's sections and pages. The conversion result explicitly states '34 sections omitted for token budget'. Major sections such as 'About the Council' (p. 2), 'What is GIS?' (p. 3), 'FY23-24 Council Highlights' (p. 5), 'Broadband Expansion' (p. 8), 'Federal Coordination' (p. 10), 'Value of GIS' (p. 12, 18), 'Hydrography Working Group' (p. 27), 'Municipal Boundary Working Group' (p. 32), and 'Future Priorities' (p. 41) are entirely absent fro
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The source contains sections such as 'About the Council', 'What is GIS?', 'FY23-24 Council Highlights', 'Statewide Coordination', 'Federal Coordination', 'Value of GIS', 'Accomplishments 2023-2024', 'Hydrography Working Group Recommendations', 'Municipal Boundary Working Group', 'NC Broadband Expansion', 'Orthoimagery Program', 'NC OneMap', 'AddressNC', 'Lidar Working Group', 'Datum Change', 'GIS Training', and 'Future Priorities'.
concerns.location: Throughout the document, specifically between heading-1 and heading-12, and after heading-13.

concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis: The 'Acronyms' section is present but is missing nearly half of its content. The list in the output ends at 'NCEM - North Carolina Emergency Management' (bottom of page 42), omitting all 14 acronyms listed on page 43 (NCLGISA through USGS). This represents an omission of approximately 30% of the items in that specific section.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The acronyms list on page 43 includes: NCLGISA, NCORR, NCGS, NGS, NGAC, NHD, NLCD, NSDI, NSGIC, OSHR, SGUC, SMAC, TAC, and USGS.
concerns.output_quote: The list in the output ends after 'NCEM - North Carolina Emergency Management'.

concerns.description: Heading count mismatch between JSON and HTML
concerns.analysis: There is a significant mismatch between the number of headings identified in the semantic JSON (16) and those rendered in the HTML (58). This discrepancy exceeds the allowed tolerance (10% or 3+ headings). This indicates that the renderer has injected a large number of headings that were not part of the original structural mapping in the JSON, likely by wrapping section titles or table headers in heading tags during the HTML generation process.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The document contains 16 headings as per the semantic.json metadata.

concerns.analysis: The source PDF contains several functional hyperlinks and URLs (e.g., on pages 7, 18, 30, 42, and 46). However, the semantic JSON only captures a single link. While the renderer appears to have recovered 17 links in the HTML (likely through text-based regex matching), the structural failure to extract these links into the JSON output represents a loss of fidelity in the primary data object.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The source document contains links on pages 7 (it.nc.gov/gicc), 18 (Value of GIS StoryMap Hub), 30 (press release), 42 (study), and 46 (https://it.nc.gov/gicc).
concerns.output_quote: The semantic.json metadata reports only 1 link.

action_items: Verify the presence and accuracy of the 34 sections reported as omitted
Reconcile the heading structure to ensure the HTML reflects the document's true semantic hierarchy
